common.noEnglishHint: This topic has no English version

pwa.title: Install .learn
pwa.body: Add the app to your device for offline access.

search.title: Search
search.subtitle: Full-text search across topics, theory, exercises, and interview questions.

empty.initialBody: Type at least 2 characters. We search topic and concept titles, theory text, exercises, and interview questions.
empty.noResultsBody: Try rephrasing or shortening the query.

home.title: Your learning workbench
home.intro: .learn is a local-first platform where each topic is a self-contained, type-safe module. Generate new topics with the lesson-forge skill, study them in the browser, fork the repo and ask your own agent for a personal curriculum.

empty.title: No topics yet
empty.hint: Ask your agent in this repo: <hint>«Use lesson-forge, add a topic on SQL»</hint>.

home.heroSub: Topics live in this repo as MDX + YAML. Run SQL and Python in the browser. Bring your own AI keys.

noMatches.title: Nothing found
noMatches.hint: No topics match the current filters. Adjust your query or reset the filters.

freeRecall.subtitle: Recall everything you can before checking
freeRecall.instructions: Write down everything you remember from this section, in your own words. Don't scroll back up.
freeRecall.confidencePrompt: How confident do you feel overall?
freeRecall.scheduledHint: A review of this concept was added to your plan

topic.failedBody: Something interrupted loading this topic. Check your connection and try again.
topic.notFoundBody: This topic isn't in the catalog. The link may be out of date.
topic.noExercises: No exercises for this concept yet.
topic.theoryMissing: Theory file {{filename}} could not be loaded.
topic.fallbackBanner: This topic is available only in {{language}}. Content is shown in the original language.

notes.label: Note
notes.hint: Saved automatically, locally in your browser.

note.label: Note
note.placeholder: A short note for this highlight…

calibration.heading: Confidence calibration
calibration.hint: Rate your confidence when answering - it shows where you overestimate or underestimate yourself
calibration.empty: No data yet: rate your confidence (sure / unsure / guessing) when answering exercises and checkpoints
calibration.byTopic: Topics where you overestimate yourself most

progress.title: Your progress
progress.subtitle: All metrics live in your browser (IndexedDB) and never leave the device.

empty.noTopicsBody: Add the first topic with lesson-forge, or browse the catalog.
empty.noProgressBody: Start with a recommended topic, and your results will show up here automatically.

topic-fully-read.title: Read to the end
topic-fully-read.description: Read every concept of one topic

three-topics-one-day.title: Day polyglot
three-topics-one-day.description: Three different topics in one day

flawless-day.title: Flawless day
flawless-day.description: Solved with no mistakes in a day

five-topics-one-day.title: Day of the polymath
five-topics-one-day.description: Five different topics in one day

well-calibrated.title: Well calibrated
well-calibrated.description: Confidence calibration score of 85%+ (30+ rated answers)

hint-free-25.title: No hints needed
hint-free-25.description: 25 exercises solved without a single hint

re-exam-graduate.title: Passed and closed
re-exam-graduate.description: A concept fully graduated from the spaced re-exam ladder

exam-ace.title: Perfect score
exam-ace.description: Passed an exam with no mistakes

weekend-warrior.title: Weekend warrior
weekend-warrior.description: Studied on both Saturday and Sunday

perfect-week.title: Perfect week
perfect-week.description: Active all 7 days of a week

retention-master.title: Retention master
retention-master.description: High retention in at least two topics

weakest.heading: Your weakest concepts
weakest.hint: Concepts where your results are lowest. Tap to jump back to the theory and drill.

data.heading: Data
data.hint: All progress is stored in this browser. Export it to a file to move it to another device or keep a backup.
data.importInvalid: This file is not a .learn export

settings.freeRecallDesc: A "recall everything" block after each concept's theory
settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: Appearance, language and your learning data. Everything is stored locally in this browser.
settings.appearanceDesc: Theme, accent color, text size and motion.
settings.accentDesc: Color of links, buttons and highlights.
settings.readingWidthDesc: Maximum line width of theory text.
settings.densityDesc: Spacing and size of interface elements.
settings.dailyGoalDesc: How many exercises or cards you want to do each day.
settings.newCardsPerDayDesc: How many new flashcards to introduce each day (0–100).
settings.sessionLimitDesc: How many cards to review in one session.
settings.targetRetentionDesc: How well you want to remember; higher means more frequent reviews.
settings.weeklyGoalDesc: Number of active days per week you aim for (1–7). Off when unset.
settings.contentLanguageDesc: Language of theory and exercises. Separate from the interface language.

editor.title: Code editor
editor.description: Defaults for the in-app code editor.
editor.tabSizeDesc: Number of spaces per indentation level.
editor.wordWrapDesc: Wrap long lines to the editor width.
editor.lineNumbersDesc: Show line numbers in the gutter.

reminders.title: Reminders
reminders.description: Local study reminders. Nothing is sent to a server.
reminders.enabledDesc: Remind you to study at a set time.
reminders.timeDesc: When to remind you each selected day.
reminders.daysDesc: Which days of the week to remind you.

reading.title: Reading
reading.description: Font, width and density of theory text.

settings.dataDesc: Progress is stored in this browser. Export it to a file to move it or keep a backup.
settings.resetConfirmHint: This deletes all attempts, cards, notes, bookmarks and highlights from this browser. API keys are not affected.
settings.resetConfirmSyncWarning: Sync is on: your data will come back from the server on the next sync. To reset progress for good, disable sync above first.

toast.importInvalid: This file is not a .learn export

sync.heading: Cross-device sync
sync.notLinkedHint: Create a code on one device and enter it on another. Progress then merges in the background automatically.
sync.codeCreatedNote: Save this code: you'll need it to link another device, and the server doesn't store it and can't recover it for you.

errors.network: Can't reach the server, try again later

status.notFoundError: code not found on the server (deleted or expired), link again

sync.unlinkConfirmBody: This device will stop sharing progress under this code.
sync.unlinkDeleteRemote: Also delete data from the server
sync.restoreDialogWarning: Restoring replaces your current local progress. If sync is active, the server's data will merge back in on the next sync, so disable sync first if that matters to you.

help.title: How this works
help.p1: Create a code on one device and enter it on another. Sync then runs automatically in the background.
help.p2: Merging combines progress from both devices; nothing is lost.
help.p3: The code is your only access key. Keep it to yourself: the server doesn't store it and can't recover it for you.
help.p4: Deleting bookmarks, notes, highlights and your own cards now syncs across devices. A full progress reset stays local.
help.p5: Inactive codes are removed from the server after about 90 days.
help.p6: Snapshots are encrypted on your device (end-to-end): the server can't read your progress.

reissue.confirmBody: A new code is created and the old one stops working immediately. Other devices will need to be linked again with the new code. Your progress isn't lost: it moves to the new code.
reissue.success: A new sync code was issued

deepLink.replaceWarning: This device is already linked to a different code. The current link will be replaced.

qr.caption: Anyone who scans this QR code gets access to your progress. Only show it to the device you're linking.

language.title: Interface language
language.description: Primary language is Russian. If your browser language isn't supported, Russian is used as fallback.

admin.title: Admin
admin.subtitle: Manage topic proposals and the visibility of existing topics. Approve to flag for materialization; reject to drop; mark materialized to link a submission to a topic that now exists.
admin.apiUnavailableDevHint: If it's not running locally, start it with pnpm dev:api.
admin.networkError: Network error talking to the API.

fields.reviewerNotePlaceholder: Optional: reason for the decision, follow-up notes…

hidden.empty: No existing topics yet, add the first one via lesson-forge.

admin.logoutAllHint: Revokes every active access and refresh token. Requires a step-up code.

login.title: Admin sign-in
login.subtitle: Login/password + a code from your authenticator app. Five failures triggers a temporary lockout.

fields.totpHint: If your authenticator is unavailable, enter a backup code in the XXXX-XXXX format.

stepUp.title: Confirm action
stepUp.subtitle: Enter a fresh authenticator code to perform «{{action}}».

proposals.title: Proposed topics
proposals.subtitle: Topics suggested by other users. Search is typo-tolerant; filter by status; jump to materialized topics directly.

errors.apiDevHint: If it's not running locally, start it with pnpm dev:api.
errors.network: Network error talking to the API.

submit.title: Suggest a topic
submit.subtitle: Sketch out what you want to learn. The maintainer will review and, if approved, the lesson-forge skill will materialize the topic.

fields.outlinePlaceholder: What should the topic cover? Which concepts, in what order, with what depth?

submit.successTitle: Thanks, your proposal is in the queue
submit.successMessage: The maintainer reviews submissions in the /admin queue. If approved, the topic is materialized via the lesson-forge skill and merged.

common.solutionLocked: Solution unlocks after all hints or 3 failed attempts.
common.solutionConfirm: Sure? This won't count as solved.

exercise.noRunner: Interactive runner for {{type}} is not implemented yet.

sql.pass: Pass: your query produced the expected rows.
sql.misordered: Hint: rows match but the order differs.
sql.noResultYet: Run the query to see rows here.

hint.stdout: enter the printed text exactly as it would appear
hint.rows: Fill in the rows the query returns (one per result row)

reasons.sql-rows-mismatch: result rows do not match the expected ones
reasons.sql-scalar-mismatch: the value does not match the expected one
reasons.predict-rows-mismatch: the predicted rows do not match the expected ones
reasons.invalid-answer: the answer has an invalid format

python.noOutput: No output yet, run your code.

init.note: Python runtime ~10 MB, downloads once, cached offline.

git.emptyLog: Run git commands and watch the graph.

boundary.title: Something went wrong
boundary.chunkTitle: Failed to load part of the app

load.title: Couldn't load
load.body: Something interrupted the load. Check your connection and try again.

runtime.title: The runtime failed
runtime.timeout: Your code ran too long and was stopped. Check for an infinite loop, then run it again.
runtime.crash: The runtime ended unexpectedly. Restart it and try again.
runtime.stalled: The runtime couldn't finish downloading. Check your connection and restart the environment.
runtime.terminated: Execution was interrupted. Run your code again.
runtime.unknown: The runtime returned an error. Restart it and try again.

heatmap.legendScale: Cell shade grows with the number of actions per day: empty means no activity, brighter means more (peak this period: {{max}}).

addTopic.suggestHint: Fill a short form. Goes to the maintainer queue for review.
addTopic.prHint: Use the lesson-forge skill locally, then push and open a PR.

onboarding.step1Body: A local-first learning workbench. Topics live in the repo as MDX and YAML, and all your progress stays in your browser only.
onboarding.step2Body: Every topic pairs theory with interactive exercises: SQL and Python run in the browser. Mark concepts read, bookmark them and take notes.
onboarding.step3Body: Press Ctrl+K to search all content, and "?" shows every keyboard shortcut. Your due cards wait on the Today tab.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Available on topic pages and during flashcard review.

pystepper.title: Step-by-step trace
pystepper.idle: Run the trace to step through execution.
pystepper.outputEmpty: Nothing printed yet at this step.
pystepper.parseError: Could not parse the trace output.

factory.label: class and instances
factory.limit: Enough: each instance clearly lives its own life.

mro.label: method lookup along the chain
mro.idle: Click a method: Python looks it up bottom to top.
mro.missing: {{method}} not found in any class: AttributeError

access.label: access levels
access.idle: Click an access attempt from outside the class and see what happens.
access.protected: technically works, but "_" signals "do not touch from outside"
access.private: AttributeError: the name is mangled into {{mangled}}

compose.rippled: a change in the base class rippled through every subclass
compose.swapped: the part was swapped, and the rest of the system never noticed

join.label: how JOIN works
join.description: A JOIN demo between tables {{leftTitle}} and {{rightTitle}} on keys {{leftKeyName}} and {{rightKeyName}}. The buttons switch the join type; the result shows which rows matched.

aggregate.label: how an aggregate works
aggregate.idle: {{count}} rows waiting to be folded - pick a function and press the button.

sandbox.clickRun: Click run to execute the query.

interview.title: Interview preparation
interview.subtitle: Explained questions grouped by topic and interview stage. Filter, search and mark what you have studied.
interview.empty: Nothing found. Adjust the filters or query.
interview.notFoundBody: There is no such question. Go back to the list.
interview.practiceSubtitle: A few tasks to confirm you understood the material, not just read it. Tasks change on each revisit.

exam.title: Exam across all topics
exam.subtitle: A collection of interactive tasks across all topics: choosing answers, writing code, predicting output and filling blanks. Set the filters and test yourself.
exam.gradingNote: Scoring reflects attempts made in this exam. Items you had already mastered count as correct when re-confirmed; items left unattempted are not counted.

readiness.low: Brush up on the material before the interview.
readiness.medium: Decent, but there are gaps - work through the review.

flashcards.title: Flashcards
flashcards.hubSubtitle: Review the key ideas of each topic with FSRS-scheduled flashcards.
flashcards.hubEmptyBody: Decks are built from topics. Open the catalog and start a topic, and its flashcards will appear here.
flashcards.interviewEmptyBody: Cards are built from the answers in the interview prep section.
flashcards.errorBody: Something interrupted loading this deck. Check your connection and try again.
flashcards.ratingGroup: Rate how well you recalled the card
flashcards.caughtUpBody: Nothing due right now. You can run all {{count}} again.
flashcards.noDeck: This topic has no flashcards yet.
flashcards.interviewEmpty: Interview flashcards are not available yet.
flashcards.modeTopicsHint: Pick multiple topics and review with FSRS scheduling.
flashcards.modeInterviewHint: Questions and answers from the interview prep section.

flashcards.coverageWarning: {{missing}} of {{total}} interview questions have no answer section, so flashcards were not generated for them.

practice.title: Flashcard practice
practice.subtitle: Pick a source, tune filters, and start a review session.
practice.noCards: No cards match the selected filters.
practice.randomSubtitle: Cards from all topics and interview prep
practice.randomHint: Cards from every topic and every interview question will be shuffled together. Good for a quick pre-interview drill.

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.subtitle: A free playground not tied to any topic. Pick a template, edit the data and run SQL or Python right in the browser. Everything persists across reloads.

gallery.choose: Pick a template to get started

sql.emptyQuery: The query is empty - write some SQL and run again.

shop.label: Online store

python.timeout: Looks like an infinite loop: execution was stopped on timeout.

dataclass.label: Classes & dataclass

regex.description: Search and replace in an orders string.

blank.label: Blank file

map.title: Knowledge map

reason.resumePlain: Pick up where you left off.

reason.unlocked: Prerequisites mastered, this topic is now open.

calibrationReview.heading: Confidence review
calibrationReview.hint: Cases where your confidence didn't match the outcome - worth reinforcing

glossary.subtitle: Key terms from every topic with short definitions. Each term links to the topic where it is covered.

tracks.title: Tracks
tracks.subtitle: Curated routes through the topics in the right order - from fundamentals to production. Pick a track for your role and follow the steps.

analytics.title: Site analytics
analytics.subtitle: Anonymous, real-time visit statistics
analytics.disabledText: Extended analytics is only available on the deployed site.
analytics.noData: Data will appear as visits come in

analytics.privacy: All data is anonymous: no cookies, no IP, aggregate counters only. The device identifier is a random local UUID.

notFound.title: Page not found

seo.homeTitle: Interactive Python, SQL, Git and web development courses
seo.homeDescription: Free interactive platform: 46 topics with theory, Python and SQL sandboxes, a git trainer, flashcards and progress tracking. Runs in the browser, no signup required.
seo.flashcardsDescription: Flashcards for every topic with FSRS spaced-repetition scheduling.
seo.glossaryDescription: A programming glossary with quick search and links to related topics.
seo.interviewDescription: Interview questions covering the course topics, for technical-interview prep.
seo.tracksDescription: Topic bundles by role: learning paths for backend, frontend and other tracks.
seo.mapDescription: A dependency map of course topics with your progress on each one.
seo.sandboxDescription: A browser Python and SQL sandbox for experiments with no environment setup.
